name: Maine Fish Chowder
recipeInstructions:
Use 3 - 3 1/4 pounds fresh skinned haddock or other white-fleshed fish with bones in it.
Fry salt pork in a heavy kettle until the fat renders completely, then remove. Add potatoes, onions, and half a teaspoon of salt. Cover with hot water and cook over medium heat for 15 minutes, covered, until the potatoes are just tender. Do not overcook.
Meanwhile, cut the fish into large chunks and add to another saucepan. Add boiling water to cover and half a teaspoon of salt. Cook slowly, covered, until the fish is fork-tender, about 15 minutes. Remove from heat.
Strain and reserve the liquid. Remove any bones from the fish. Add the fish and strained liquid to the potato-onion mixture. Pour in milk and leave on the stove for 5 minutes to heat through. Mix in butter and pepper. Serve immediately. Serves 4 to 6.
